original text:
Today would like talk about another unique embroidery tradition Dnister corridor, This river separates historic districts Pokuttia Podillia. This region incredibly rich embroidery techniques. This technique mostly known being used ends ancient woman's headpiece, peremitka, which long narrow piece linen which wound around head neck married women. ends this garment were always decorated either with woven design with embroidery. These ornemented ends were called 'zabir'. edges were sometimes also ornamented, usually with simple woven designs. Here some women Horoden'ka district costume wearing peremitky.
This type embroidery used women's chemises well, lasted longest zabory because could made look almost same both sides.
image article good example this technique, even though photo somewhat washed out. will using several images which rather poor quality, apologize that able find better.
framework these designs laid black cross stich, sometimes stem stich. zabir, double sided cross stitch used. Then colors were filled with pine type stitch, which started from center line outlined area extended diagonally outer edge. This often executed silk thread, sometimes metallic thread outlines, often decorative elements edge design were done with Holbein stitches were used edging, including buttonhole chain stitch.
Elements which looking almost like eyelets, they pulled open. primary colors used dark reds, edging into maroon purple with other colors used accents.
Some designs stem stitch instead cross stitch, filling done same however.
Many designs about diagonal axis, which similar embroidery designs Balkans.
course, wherever creative existed, variations technique also existed.
More recently, this technique, with many others, imitated with Here shoulder insets from women's chemises with same design, done rozchokhy, other cross stitch.
another pair, from zabory.
fact, belief that many designs this area with strong black outlines individual motifs which today only found cross stitch were originally executed rozchokha. would reverse engineer these designs.
suppose should mention that some these designs were left unfinished more clearly show structure.
will remnant rozchokha cross stitch design.
This technique today been revived, being taught places like L'viw. designs using technique also being invented. such piece personal collection, borrowed never returned.
This beautiful part creative heritage Ukraine world, would encourage This technique great deal potential.
Thank reading. will close with some more images pieces done this technique.
Hello, Roman. Thanks this post! thought, that Pokuttya peremitkas mainly woven (like this from your post opened eyes wonderful local version peremitkas. don't have Iryna Svyontek's book, you, there book this embroidery?
way, "some women Horoden'ka district costume wearing peremitky" folk band "Peremitka" from village Luka, Horodenka raion watch their youtube
Thank you, Nawko, would like know where could find other books. have found them available here.
does show this embroidery, save enlarge black white images able done.It pretty cross stitch outline stitch, filling with sosnytsia. Most books Ukrainian embroidery cover this, example Diakiw neil. called plait stitch, show stib, somesuch. There diagrams 'Podil'ska Narodna Vyshywka Liudmyla
Thank your please know could other books.
Перші присвячені Гуцульщині, одну книжкою привезли іншу найстаршу скановану копію форматі djvu. Києві видно продажу, видно
Нагадують книжку Сороханюк, жаль, гіршої якості. маєте знати, якість книжок пані Свйонтек кожним виданням маєте найкраща, давніші трохи
знайшла видало трьох книжок, "Нова Зоря", ними можна поштою nz@com.ua.
Також, ймовірно, книжки авторки, контактів нажаль маю.
один коментар усе, хочу, погана англійська дозволить написати (сподіваюсь, мене зрозумієте). білі вишивки Суємці навколишні) шукаю книги статті, виділяли вишивку, поки марно. Єдине джерело, містить замальовки народний орнамент" Олени Пчілки,  (починаючи аркуша). Завдяки Тетяні можемо видання онлайн альбом містить зразки орнаментів повіту, якого район належав, містить конкретики щодо місць кожного окремого узору також мість дуже загальний опис вишивки виділення якихось локальних рис.
інше, вдалось знайти фото вишивок Мережі. Велику підбірку фондах музею Гончара пані Олена Уткіна. фото можна знайти альбомі іншої пані Лідії Бебешко, перетворює PM-схеми:
мене зацікавило біле шитво, оремий різновид вишивок білим білому. скільки бачу воно якраз мало схоже вінницьке снятинське, більше схоже полтавське, хоча якраз пунктиками хоча мало бути більше схоже рівненське район), туди ближче.
majete uvazi Podil'ske Tak, maju kil'ky fotohrafii tseho takozh. posylaty jakscho meni daiete adress email.
exhaustive articles here,thank you!
very modern view things have. Embroidery Ukraine many other places considered essential. was, some extent still visible mark your good taste, are, evidence proper skills. Untill about 's, cloth thread items were made home, hand. Today exorbitant prices good linen, those days, much more available. Even when commercial thread began used, never that expensive. Secondly, these clothes were worn season then discarded, today. Linen extremely durable, garment could worn decades. This especially true most garments which were only worn important occasions, perhaps church Sunday, most. Everyday clothes would eventually wear out, less embroidery, even then would recycled footcloths, baby swaddling like. This much done city women untill 's when became have ethnic identity. woman villages worked family farm, much year work does leave with great deal free time, excepting only planting time harvest. Also, most important things that young girls were expected prepare their trousseau; girl considered ready marriage untill proper collection clothes household linens prepared, often including shirt future spouse. Women would also embroider shirts their husbands sons. considered extremely strong protective talisman young into world with shirt mother. Embroidery never considered just hobby', formed vital part life. This completely been lost Ukrainian culture even today. course, this applies many other peoples well.
Ohh,thank you, Roman; very
Hi.. Great post. Well have discussed here things about Costume Embroidery your images also looks amazing. Keep this type posting.
